nutrition.elevationAdjust: Carbs and sodium adjusted +{{pct}}% for hilly course
nutrition.carry.singleBottle: A single 500ml bottle is enough between aid stations.
nutrition.carry.softFlask: Carry a 500ml soft flask — refill at every aid station.
nutrition.carry.twoBottles: Carry two 500ml bottles or a 1L vest bladder.
nutrition.carry.vest1_5: A 1.5L hydration vest covers ~2 hours between aid stations.
nutrition.carry.vest2: A 2L hydration vest or pack is recommended for this distance.
training.title: Race day vs. what you buy
training.gelOverage_one: Your race needs <bold>{{race}} gel</bold>. Sold in multi-packs, your cart includes <bold>{{cart}} gels</bold> — the extra <bold>{{extra}}</bold> are perfect for training.
training.gelOverage_other: Your race needs <bold>{{race}} gels</bold>. Sold in multi-packs, your cart includes <bold>{{cart}} gels</bold> — the extra <bold>{{extra}}</bold> are perfect for training.
training.barOverage_one: Your cart also includes an extra <bold>{{extra}} bar</bold> for training.
training.barOverage_other: Your cart also includes an extra <bold>{{extra}} bars</bold> for training.
training.barOverageOnly_one: Your cart includes an extra <bold>{{extra}} bar</bold> for training.
training.barOverageOnly_other: Your cart includes an extra <bold>{{extra}} bars</bold> for training.
training.prepTitle: How to use them for race prep
training.tip1: Practice with gels on long runs 3–4 weeks before your race to train your gut
training.tip2: Replicate your race-day fuelling schedule during training (same timing, same products)
training.tip3: Start with 1 gel per hour and build up to your target race frequency
training.tip4: Never try a new product on race day — always test in training first

tryPro.title: Get a more precise plan
tryPro.body: The Pro planner personalises every number to your exact weight, fitness level, and conditions. It also adds elevation data, gut training mode, a detailed race timeline, and checkpoint planning.
quick.caveat: Estimated for a 70 kg intermediate athlete at race pace. Your actual targets may vary by 20–40% depending on your weight, fitness, and effort level.
quick.upsellTitle: Get exact targets for your body
quick.upsellBody: Pro plan uses your actual weight, fitness level, elevation, and effort to personalise every number. Also unlocks gut training mode, checkpoint planning, coach notes, and support for other nutrition brands alongside Lecka.
quick.preciseCta: Get my precise plan — takes 3 minutes →
products.realFoodNote: Real food foundation — gut-friendly base for your race.
gutTraining.intro: {{days}} days to race day. Here's how to train your gut to absorb {{carbs}}g carbs/hour comfortably.
gutTraining.nauseaTitle: If you feel nausea in training
gutTraining.nauseaBody: Drop one gel and hold that level for two runs before building again. Never race at a carb load you haven't practised.
gutTraining.raceWeekBody: Stick to exactly what you've trained with. No new products. Keep gel timing consistent on your final shakeout run.
gutTraining.noDate: Add your race date to see a personalised gut training protocol.
